metrics.narrative: No quantified business problem or ROI case documented. SOQL Q3 synthesis-map fields (Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Value_to_Customer__c, Compelling_Event__c, What_if_this_is_not_done__c, Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c, Gaps_identified_during_sales_demo__c) are all null. The only evidence of buyer intent is the RFP request itself (Next_Step__c 2026-06-23), which signals Lincoln Financial is evaluating S2P platforms but does not reveal the underlying pain, cost impact, cycle-time problem, or savings target driving the initiative. On a $250k+ S2P deal, the buyer typically has a quantified procurement-efficiency or spend-under-management goal (e.g., '15% cycle-time reduction', 'consolidate 4 legacy tools into one platform', '$2M annual savings from contract compliance'), but none is captured here. Without a quantified value case, Zycus cannot anchor the RFP response to measurable outcomes or differentiate on ROI. The move is to run a discovery call within 7 days (before the RFP is issued) to surface the CFO or procurement VP's business case, quantify the pain, and position iSource/iSupplier/eProcurement/Agentic AI against those metrics in the RFP response.

champion.narrative: No champion identified. Zero OpportunityContactRoles (SOQL ContactRole query returned 0 rows), so no Champion or Coach is mapped. SOQL Q3 Champion synthesis-map fields (X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c, Decision_Maker_Name_Title__c, Decision_Maker_Identified__c) are all null. No Avoma calls to surface who internally advocates for Zycus, who has access to the Economic Buyer, or who will coach Zycus on what the evaluation committee values. The only buyer contact referenced is Jordan Bullock (eSourcing Consultant, Task 2026-06-23), but role and advocacy level are unknown: is Jordan a gatekeeper screening vendors, or a strategic sourcing lead who can influence the RFP rubric and push Zycus to the shortlist? On an RFP deal, a champion is critical: they share draft evaluation criteria before the RFP is issued, coach Zycus on what the committee prioritizes (price vs AI innovation vs integration ease), introduce Zycus to the Economic Buyer, and advocate internally to push the Zycus response to the top of the shortlist ahead of competitors. Without a champion, Zycus is responding blind to a buyer-controlled process and has no insider intelligence or internal push. Competitors with established relationships at Lincoln Financial (e.g., SAP Ariba if SAP ERP is in place, Coupa if they have a foothold in another business unit, Jaggaer if they are the incumbent) will have champions coaching them, giving Zycus a structural disadvantage. The move is to identify a champion candidate during the pre-RFP discovery call (look for someone passionate about solving the S2P/AI problem, frustrated with the status quo, and open to collaboration), build their advocacy through value-add touches (benchmarks, peer references, use-case wins), and secure their commitment to coach Zycus and introduce the Economic Buyer before the RFP response is due.

competition.narrative: Competition unknown. SOQL Q3 competitive synthesis-map fields (Competitors__c, Others_Competitors_Please_specify__c, How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c, Existing_vendor__c, Replacing_What__c, Moved_To__c, Why_not_Zycus__c, Zycus_Differentiation_Why_Zycus__c, Closed_Lost_Reason_Code__c) are all null. No Avoma calls to surface which vendors Lincoln Financial is benchmarking (likely SAP Ariba if SAP ERP is in place, Coupa if they have a digital-procurement initiative, Jaggaer or Ivalua if they are the incumbent, GEP if cost-focused). RFP deals are inherently competitive (Lincoln Financial is inviting multiple vendors to respond), but Zycus has zero intel on who else is in the mix, what their relationships are (incumbent? existing footprint in another BU? executive sponsorship?), or how the buyer perceives them. This is a fatal blind spot: if Ariba is entrenched and the RFP rubric weights ERP-native integration heavily, Zycus must counter with S2P workflow depth and AI/Agentic differentiation in the response. If Coupa has a champion and is positioning on ease-of-use, Zycus must lean into enterprise scale and configurability. If Jaggaer is the incumbent and the pain is 'Jaggaer is too rigid', Zycus must highlight flexibility and modern UX. Without knowing the competition, Zycus cannot craft a differentiated RFP response or a wedge narrative. The move is to discover the competitive set during the pre-RFP call (ask Jordan Bullock: 'Who else are you evaluating? What do you like about them? What concerns do you have?'), map each competitor's strength and weakness, and position iSource/iSupplier/eProcurement/Agentic AI as the unique answer to the buyer's top pain (e.g., 'Unlike Ariba's rigid ERP-centric workflows, Zycus iSource gives you configurable S2P automation that adapts to your procurement ops, plus Merlin agentic AI that negotiates supplier pricing autonomously—capabilities Ariba and Coupa do not offer').

Pre-RFP positioning window is open but closing fast. Lincoln Financial has executed the NDA and is preparing to release the RFP (Next_Step__c 2026-06-23). Once the RFP is issued, Zycus can only respond to the criteria as written and cannot shape what the buyer values. The highest-leverage move this week is to request a discovery call before the RFP drops, framed as 'ensuring our response addresses your top priorities.' Use this call to surface pain, map the committee, identify the Economic Buyer and champion, and subtly position iSource/iSupplier/eProcurement/Agentic AI strengths (e.g., AI-driven negotiation, supplier collaboration, spend visibility) as must-haves in the evaluation rubric. This call converts a blind RFP response into a coached, champion-backed submission.

Zero buyer-side contact roles create single-thread risk. The opportunity has 0 OpportunityContactRoles, despite 20+ procurement and finance contacts at the account level (including VP Global Procurement, Finance Transformation VP, CFO Annuities, Life CFO). A recent Task references a buyer individual, but that person is not in a contact role and their influence (champion, gatekeeper, or passive coordinator) is unconfirmed. On an RFP deal, losing the single contact or misreading their authority kills the deal. The move is to use the discovery call to ask 'Who else should be involved in this conversation?' and 'Who reviews vendor responses?', then add those stakeholders as OpportunityContactRoles and reach them directly (multi-thread). Target: map and engage at least 3-4 buyer-side roles (procurement ops, strategic sourcing, IT/integration lead, Economic Buyer) by the time the RFP response is due.

AI/Agentic positioning must be validated early. The product scope includes 'Agentic AI' (source: SF Products__c), but AIS_Score__c and AIS_Status__c are null, so Lincoln Financial's true AI appetite is unknown. If they are AI Hungry (a procurement transformation mandate, a cost-reduction target that requires automation), Merlin/I2O (agentic negotiation, autonomous sourcing) is the wedge that differentiates Zycus from Coupa/Ariba/GEP. If they are AI Curious or Resistant, leading with Agentic AI risks sounding futuristic and unproven. The discovery call must gauge their AI maturity: Are they already using AI in procurement? Do they have an enterprise AI strategy? What does 'Agentic AI' mean to them (automation, or true autonomous decision-making)? Then tailor the RFP response narrative—lead with Merlin if they are hungry, lead with proven S2P workflow (iSource/iSupplier) with AI as a Phase 2 upside if they are cautious.

Secure pre-RFP discovery call within 7 days to shape the evaluation criteria. The buyer issued an NDA for an RFP on 2026-06-23 (4 days ago) and Zycus is awaiting the RFP document. Once the RFP is issued, Zycus can only respond to the criteria as written and cannot influence what the buyer values. If the RFP is written to favor a competitor's strengths (e.g., SAP Ariba for ERP-native integration, Coupa for broad P2P coverage, GEP for services-led transformation), Zycus will be disadvantaged. The pre-RFP discovery call is the only window to position iSource/iSupplier/eProcurement/Agentic AI capabilities as must-haves (e.g., AI-driven supplier negotiation, tail-spend automation, cognitive sourcing) and to coach the buyer on what differentiated criteria to include. Request a discovery call with Jordan Bullock (eSourcing Consultant) or the procurement lead by 2026-07-04 (7 days), ask what problems the S2P initiative is solving, what the evaluation committee cares most about, and who else is being considered, then use that insight to shape the RFP criteria (if possible) or tailor the response to the buyer's priorities. If the RFP is issued before Zycus can influence it, the deal will be a commodity bake-off decided on price or incumbent advantage.

Map the Economic Buyer and approval chain before the RFP response is due. No Economic Buyer has been identified (0 buyer-side OpportunityContactRoles; account-level contacts include VP Global Procurement, Finance Transformation VP, CFO Annuities, Life CFO, but none are confirmed involved in this opp). For a $250k+ S2P platform investment, the EB is typically a VP or SVP in Procurement, Finance, or Operations who controls the multi-year SaaS budget and signs off on the business case. Without EB identification and alignment, the deal will stall post-RFP at the proposal stage—procurement ops can shortlist Zycus, but they cannot approve the spend. The RFP process may obscure the EB (procurement issues the RFP, but finance or a C-level sponsor holds the budget). During the pre-RFP discovery call (requested by 2026-07-04), ask who will approve the budget and sign the contract, and secure a direct line to that person (or their proxy) before the RFP response is submitted. If the EB is not mapped by the time Zycus is shortlisted, a competitor with better executive sponsorship will win.

Build a champion on the evaluation committee within the next 14 days. No champion has been identified (0 buyer-side OpportunityContactRoles; only buyer contact is Jordan Bullock, eSourcing Consultant, likely RFP coordinator not decision-maker). On an RFP deal, a champion coaches Zycus on what the committee values, shares draft evaluation criteria, highlights which competitors are being considered, and pushes the Zycus response to the top of the shortlist. Without a champion, Zycus is responding blind and cannot differentiate from competitors with insider relationships. Engage Jordan Bullock immediately (by 2026-06-30, within 3 days) to assess whether Jordan has influence and access to the evaluation committee. If Jordan is a coordinator, ask to be introduced to the procurement lead, VP Procurement, or a business sponsor who can provide strategic context. If a potential champion is identified (someone passionate about solving the S2P/AI problem with access to decision-makers), invest in building their advocacy through value-add touches: industry benchmarks, peer references (financial services S2P customers), use-case demos (Agentic AI for supplier negotiation), and insights that help them make the internal business case for Zycus. If no champion is developed by the time the RFP is submitted, Zycus will be one of several generic vendors and will not make the finalist shortlist.

Without one, Zycus responds blind and cannot differentiate from competitors with insider relationships. The move is to identify a champion candidate during pre-RFP discovery (someone passionate about solving S2P/AI problem, frustrated with status quo) and build advocacy through value-add touches before RFP response is due.
